result:
## e1000e Driver — Build-System Gating Chain

**(a) Kconfig option**

`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58`
```
config E1000E
	tristate "Intel(R) PRO/1000 PCI-Express Gigabit Ethernet support"
	depends on PCI && (!SPARC32 || BROKEN)
	depends on PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL
	select CRC32
```
(companion option `E1000E_HWTS` at line 78, `depends on E1000E && X86`, not required for the base driver.)

**(b) Makefile rule(s) compiling e1000e**

`drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8`
```
obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/
```
descends into `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11-15`:
```
obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o

e1000e-objs := 82571.o ich8lan.o 80003es2lan.o \
	       mac.o manage.o nvm.o phy.o \
	       param.o ethtool.o netdev.o ptp.o
```

**(c) All gating layers, root → leaf**

Kconfig chain (each `source` line is nested inside the previous file's `if <PARENT_OPT>` block):

| Layer | File:line | Statement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `drivers/net/Kconfig:6` | `menuconfig NETDEVICES` — `depends on NET` |
| 1 | `drivers/net/Kconfig:27` | `if NETDEVICES` (opens block containing line 490) |
| 1 | `drivers/net/Kconfig:490` | `source "drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig"` |
| 1 | `drivers/net/Kconfig:647` | `endif # NETDEVICES` |
| 2 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:6` | `menuconfig ETHERNET` — `depends on NET` |
| 2 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig` (`if ETHERNET`) | opens block containing line 86 |
| 2 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Kconfig:86` | `source "drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig"` |
| 3 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:6` | `config NET_VENDOR_INTEL` — `bool`, `default y` |
| 3 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:17` | `if NET_VENDOR_INTEL` |
| 3 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:58` | `config E1000E` (target option) |
| 3 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Kconfig:359` | `endif # NET_VENDOR_INTEL` |

⚠️ Note: I could not find who sources `drivers/net/Kconfig` — grepping the whole checkout for `"drivers/net/Kconfig"` returns nothing. That's because this working copy is a **trimmed kernel tree**: the top-level `Kconfig` and `drivers/Kconfig` (which normally do `source "drivers/net/Kconfig"` under the "Device Drivers" menu) are both absent here (confirmed: `find . -maxdepth 2 -iname Kconfig` only shows `net/Kconfig` and `scripts/kconfig`). So the chain above is complete for what exists in this checkout; the missing top link is a property of the partial repo, not a driver-specific gate.

Makefile chain (root → leaf), each `obj-$(CONFIG_X) += dir/` line causes descent into the next Makefile:

| Layer | File:line | Statement |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | `drivers/Makefile:95` | `obj-y += net/` (unconditional descent into `drivers/net/`) |
| 2 | `drivers/net/Makefile:52` | `obj-$(CONFIG_ETHERNET) += ethernet/` |
| 3 | `drivers/net/ethernet/Makefile:50` | `obj-$(CONFIG_NET_VENDOR_INTEL) += intel/` |
| 4 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/Makefile:8` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e/` |
| 5 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:11` | `obj-$(CONFIG_E1000E) += e1000e.o` |
| 5 | `drivers/net/ethernet/intel/e1000e/Makefile:13-15` | `e1000e-objs := 82571.o ich8lan.o 80003es2lan.o mac.o manage.o nvm.o phy.o param.o ethtool.o netdev.o ptp.o` |

Same caveat applies: the link from the top-level `Makefile` into `drivers/Makefile` normally goes through the generic kbuild `core-y`/`drivers-y` mechanism (`Makefile:746-747,1111,1113`), but with `drivers/Kconfig` absent this is a trimmed tree, so I confirmed `drivers/Makefile` exists and is unconditionally entered (`obj-y += net/`) rather than tracing the exact top-level descent logic further (it's generic Kbuild machinery, not driver-specific gating).

Effectively, to build `e1000e.o` the kernel config needs: `NET=y/m`, `NETDEVICES=y`, `ETHERNET=y`, `NET_VENDOR_INTEL=y`, and `E1000E=y/m` (with `PCI` and `PTP_1588_CLOCK_OPTIONAL` satisfied, `CRC32` auto-selected).
